lede: A look at September 16 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Toby Couchman and Sabrina Bryan, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Washington Navy Yard shooting
events.desc: A gunman kills twelve people at the Washington Navy Yard in Washington, D.C.

events.title: Nisour Square massacre
events.desc: Security guards working for Blackwater Worldwide shoot and kill 17 Iraqis in Nisour Square, Baghdad.

events.title: East Germany balloon escape
events.desc: Eight people escape from East Germany to the west in a homemade hot air balloon.

events.title: Mikoyan MiG-31
events.desc: The first prototype of the Mikoyan MiG-31 interceptor makes its maiden flight.

events.title: Malaysia
events.desc: Malaysia is formed from the Federation of Malaya, Singapore, North Borneo (Sabah) and Sarawak. However, Singapore is soon expelled from this new country.

events.title: Typhoon Nancy (1961)
events.desc: Typhoon Nancy, with possibly the strongest winds ever measured in a tropical cyclone, makes landfall in Osaka, Japan, killing 173 people.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Italian troops conquer Sidi Barrani.

events.title: Wall Street bombing
events.desc: The Wall Street bombing: A bomb in a horse wagon explodes in front of the J. P. Morgan building in New York City killing 38 and injuring 400.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: The Battle of Harlem Heights is fought.

events.title: Owain Glyndŵr
events.desc: Owain Glyndŵr is declared Prince of Wales by his followers.

births.name: Robert Llewellyn Bradshaw
births.line: Caribbean politician, 1st Prime Minister of Saint Kitts and Nevis (died 1978)

deaths.text: Robert Redford, American actor, producer and director
Kurt Oppelt, Austrian figure skater and coach
Zsuzsa Körmöczy, Hungarian tennis player and coach
